title: swift-institute repository metadata schema
description: Per-repo .github/metadata.yaml schema. Validates the declarative GitHub-side metadata that swift-institute's sync-metadata workflow propagates to each repo's GitHub settings. Topics are per-repo editorial choices: the schema validates SHAPE (kebab-case, length, count) but NOT vocabulary — each repo establishes its own topic set based on its content. The closed-enum constraint introduced in Wave 2b was retracted 2026-05-10 per principal direction; see Skills/github-repository/SKILL.md [GH-REPO-021].

description.description: Repo's GitHub description. Class-templated per Skills/github-repository [GH-REPO-011].

topics.description: GitHub-side topic tags propagated to the repo. Per-repo editorial — each repo establishes its own topic set based on its content. The schema validates SHAPE only (kebab-case, length, uniqueness, count cap); vocabulary is open. The `swift-` prefix is conventionally avoided on the org's own repos because it repeats the org name, but the schema does not enforce that.

items.description: Lowercase kebab-case, ≤ 50 chars per [GH-REPO-022]. No closed vocabulary.

homepage.description: Repo homepage URL. Class-templated per [GH-REPO-030]. Personal URLs are forbidden per [GH-REPO-031].

settings.description: GitHub repo settings overrides. Sync-metadata workflow propagates these via the GitHub API. Hub-repo override pattern documented at [GH-REPO-094].

discussion.description: Centralized discussion-thread tracking metadata per [GH-REPO-090..093]. Authored by sync-discussion-threads-nightly.yml; consumed by validate-readme.yml when verifying [README-040] discussion-link blocks.

socialPreview.description: Social-preview rendering parameters per Skills/social-preview [SOC-002 / SOC-003].

readme.description: README routing metadata. Per Wave 2b finalization Decision 7, the `family` field drives validate-readme.yml's per-family rule subset selection.

family.description: README family per Skills/readme/SKILL.md `## Family Routing`. A=user profile, C=process/workflow repo, E=sub-package (Tier 1+ implemented Swift package), F=placeholder/reservation, G=org profile (3 sub-tiers: top-level / org-of-orgs / leaf). The taxonomy has only 5 families — B and D are NOT defined. validate-readme.yml selects the per-family rule subset based on this field.
